...an authoritative source.
Yeah, exactly.

Tools, solutions, solutions, yeah.

Box aside, it does feel like we probably should be doing this just, again, out of necessity.
Yeah, yeah.
And, you know, talking about the black box point, you know, you can prompt LLMs to be less biased or, you know, give you a list that you would prefer.

Or, like, the main ones are those, you know, those first.
Let me see if I can find one here.
These are actually homepages.
I suppose good to see in a way.
Yeah, so here you go, right?
Defendify with one of their listicles, and I'm sure that they've positioned themselves nicely in the listicle itself.
So I sympathize with your sentiment here.
I think, unfortunately, this is the way the game is played right now.
And we can, so to answer your question, yes, we can definitely put together something that you can share internally.
Okay.
What's interesting is, and a good message here is that we have found that in order to be cited, it can't just be positive spin, right?
So some listicles will just say, list a set of features for the topic.
And it'll all be very positive.
listicles a for for

actually, one theme that we found helpful is actually adding in some drawbacks.

So as a way of spinning it internally, you can also say there is absolutely value in enable giving its two cents here.

There is absolutely value in taking a critical lens on it as well.
That might help motivate internally.
might not.

We do have a couple of ideas in here.
We

I'll find one that I think is a good one to go after, and we'll draft that for you.
Okay.

I mean, I think this is, like, check that does a pretty good job of getting the message across here.
But, yeah, if you want to, unfortunately, especially in these evaluation level prompts, then list schools are the name of the game for the time being.

Yeah.
And it's, like, bananas to me.

But, yeah, in the past, I have not paid them any mind, because I figured most when they're assessing a SERP for the links they want to click on, they would realize that, you know, if you're evaluating software.

So let me put something together.
I'll share that with you guys next week.
Yeah, leave it with me.
Okay, I appreciate it, Matt.
Thank you.
Cool, 100%.

So worth just glancing there, but I'd love your view on some of the prompts.
So if you go in and group by tags, it will be easier to manage.
Just take a look because you might see, oh, hey, we're not, we're not, this prompt isn't actually relevant to us, or this one is.
I just want to make sure that we're able to like, you know, basically help you guys tell them.
Message internally, like, we're now ranking or we're cited in this specific prompt, which is incredibly valuable to the various different business groups.
Righty.
Is there anything else top of mind?
We've got five minutes, so.
I'll just say.
Yes.
Yeah, sorry.
Just really quickly, because I spoke with the UEM team last week and received feedback on how the blogs were being written.
So just one feedback from them is if we can avoid being too rigid with how we describe our product.
So I know most of it is from the, you know, the context that they provided, right?
So, like, every time N-Centralis mentioned...
And there's this corresponding description of like automation scripts.
It's like, it's very, it's the same with all of the blogs being written.
So yeah, just one feedback is if they can be more dynamic.
And for example, if the article is about, for example, endpoint automation, maybe frame the product description based on what is the topic, right?
If the topic is about, yeah, network segmentation, for example, then can we get more reference about network segmentation from the product?
Yeah.
Perfect.
I'll incorporate it.
I understand.
And we can definitely add variation that at some time is the product about this.
So we should be able to fix that.
No problem.
Cool.
And then just got a couple minutes.
So I just wanted to sense check with you guys.
Like we're almost.
Halfway through our engagement.
just wanted to take a minute just to like check in with you in terms of like giving us a kind of a grade at this point, like how you're feeling with the partnership.
Are you seeing what you wanted to see?
Is there anything we can be doing better at this point?
Like any ideas there?
And again, I don't want to put you on the spot.
So if you do have thoughts, you can get back to me next week.
But anything like, how are you feeling overall?
Yeah, I mean, I'm feeling like it's, we're getting what we thought we were going to get in terms of just the level of content and what we're producing.
You know, per most things in most partnerships that we enter with the third party agencies, we're always the holdup.
I mean, we just have entirely too much going on all the time.
And to be quite honest with you, enable lax focus.
So a lot of the things, like a lot of hiccups, they're on our end as far as like time of getting blogs out on how quickly we can get blogs out and all that stuff.
So I'm happy, you know, we're getting what I thought we would be getting from this partnership and it seems like the blog quality is pretty good in terms of what we were looking for.
Especially, you know, once we were able to go through the calibration round and get all that stuff finalized, seems like there's not a ton of edits that need to happen.
Maybe I'm wrong about that since I'm not in it day in and day out, but it feels good.
Glad to hear it.
Excellent.
Well, yeah, I think you guys share anything different there?
I think guys I I think you you
Now, on top of this, just appreciate the partnership, Matt, and yeah, we'll continue to try and get, you know, our processes buttoned up internally so that we can, you know, publish more frequently.

I'll make sure it's up, man.
I appreciate it.

And yeah, see you shortly.

Thanks, man.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content strategy, production, and new AI visibility tools.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=254.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Strategy Validated:** The current focus on AdLumen and full-stack topics is correct for N-able's cyber resiliency pivot. Future priority will shift to Cove (backup/DR) to build market awareness.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=426.0)
  - [**New Content Format:** GrowthX will draft a listicle to demonstrate the format's value for AI citation, which is now critical for appearing in evaluation-level search prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=865.0)
  - [**Editorial Process Clarified:** The workflow for approving blog topics was defined: N-able reviews topics in the "Considering" column, and GrowthX moves approved ones to "Approve to Start."](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
  - [**AI Visibility Tool:** N-able will review the "Check that" tool to validate its tracked prompts, ensuring it accurately measures relevant AI citations for internal reporting.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1459.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy & Performance

  - [**Current Mix:** Heavy focus on AdLumen (6 blogs) and full-stack topics (5 blogs) to support the company's cyber resiliency pivot.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=426.0)
  - [**Future Focus:** Shift priority to Cove (backup/DR) over UEM.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=477.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The market knows N-able for UEM but not for security or Cove. This shift aims to build awareness in new, strategic areas.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=501.0)
  - [**Performance:** AdLumen content has lower traffic, which is expected for a new product cluster. Traffic is projected to grow as more content is published and market education increases.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)

### Editorial Process & Feedback

  - [**MDR Topics:** Jen (Security PMM) provided feedback on some MDR topics.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
      - [**Action:** GrowthX will address feedback on specific topics and move all others to "Approve to Start" to begin production.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=723.0)
  - [**Product Descriptions:** UEM team feedback requested more dynamic product descriptions.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1526.0)
      - [**Problem:** Repetitive, generic descriptions (e.g., "N-Central... automation scripts") are used across all blogs.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1526.0)
      - [**Solution:** Frame product references to the specific article's topic (e.g., network segmentation) for greater relevance.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1526.0)

### New Content Format: Listicles for AI Citation

  - [**Problem:** ChatGPT frequently cites biased listicles from competitors (e.g., SentinelOne, Acronis) as authoritative sources for evaluation-level prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=865.0)
  - [**Rationale for Adoption:** This format is now a necessity to compete for AI citation, despite internal reservations.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=865.0)
  - [**Key Finding:** Listicles with a balanced view (including drawbacks) are more likely to be cited. This provides a strategic angle for N-able to offer a valuable, critical perspective.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1240.0)

### AI Visibility Tool: "Check that"

  - [**Purpose:** Track AI citations and prompt performance to demonstrate value to internal teams.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1495.0)
  - [**Action:** N-able to review the tool's tracked prompts to ensure they are relevant and accurate for measuring success.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1459.0)

### Partnership Health Check

  - [**N-able Feedback:** The partnership is meeting expectations.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1621.0)
  - [**Primary Blocker:** Internal N-able processes and lack of focus, not GrowthX's performance.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1647.0)
  - [**GrowthX Feedback:** Traffic is increasing immediately with each new blog, creating strong momentum.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1771.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
      - [Draft a listicle example for internal N-able review.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
      - [Address Jen's feedback on MDR topics and move approved ones to "Approve to Start."](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=723.0)
      - [Implement more dynamic product descriptions in future content.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1526.0)
  - [**N-able:**](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1459.0)
      - [Review prompts in the "Check that" tool to validate relevance.](https://fathom.video/share/JHRAnKwR9myTbxgGVHN3DFVWHZJ-Lt1X?tab=summary&timestamp=1459.0)

action_items.description: Review Jen's MDR feedback; update topics; set un-commented to Approved to start
Draft N-able listicle (e.g., Top endpoint management solutions); send to Andrew next week
Review Checkthat prompts; add/remove tags; share feedback w/ Matt next week
Review Checkthat prompts; add/remove tags; share feedback w/ Matt next week
Review Checkthat prompts; add/remove tags; share feedback w/ Matt next week
Update product descriptions to be topic-specific in future blogs
